CREATE TABLE employees (id INT, name TEXT, department TEXT);
SELECT department, sys.group_concat(name, ', ') FROM employees GROUP BY department;
CREATE TABLE projects (project_id INT, employee_id INT, project_name TEXT);
INSERT INTO projects VALUES (1, 1, 'Project A'), (2, 2, 'Project B'), (3, 3, 'Project A'), (4, 4, 'Project C'), (5, 5, 'Project B');
SELECT e.department, sys.group_concat(p.project_name, ' | ') FROM employees e JOIN projects p ON e.id = p.employee_id GROUP BY e.department;
SELECT e.department, sys.group_concat(DISTINCT p.project_name, '|') FROM employees e JOIN projects p ON e.id = p.employee_id WHERE e.department IS NOT NULL GROUP BY e.department;

SELECT e.department, sys.group_concat(name, ', ') FROM (SELECT e.name, e.department FROM employees e WHERE e.department IN (SELECT DISTINCT department FROM employees WHERE department IS NOT NULL)) AS e GROUP BY e.department;
SELECT sys.group_concat((SELECT name FROM employees WHERE id = 1), ', ') AS concatenated_names;
SELECT sys.group_concat(name, ' - ') FROM (SELECT name FROM employees WHERE department = 'IT' ORDER BY name) AS it_employees;
SELECT sys.group_concat((SELECT name FROM employees WHERE department = 'HR'), ', ') FROM employees WHERE id <= 3;
